name: I keep hearing about structured data generation. What’s the very first thing I should do to get started?
description: First, define *why* you need structured data (SEO, synthetic test data, dashboards, or AI search) and sketch a simple schema for your most important entities. T

mainEntity.name: I keep hearing about structured data generation. What’s the very first thing I should do to get started?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Start by clarifying *why* you need structured data—SEO, analytics, synthetic data for testing, or AI search—then define a simple schema and test generation on a small subset before scaling.
Jumping straight into tools without a clear data model is the fastest way to generate unusable or non-compliant data.

Key factors:
- Goal: SEO rich results, synthetic test data, dashboards, or AI features
- Inventory of existing data sources and fields
- Choice of schema standard (internal data model vs. schema.org)
- Validation plan (data quality checks, rich-results tests)

Practically, sketch your data model on one page (entities, fields, relationships), identify 1–2 highest-value use cases, and run a pilot with a limited dataset.
Only after you can validate the output reliably should you automate generation across your site or database.
If you’re under time pressure, consider a tool or expert to help define your schema and set up validation early.
acceptedAnswer.description: First, define *why* you need structured data (SEO, synthetic test data, dashboards, or AI search) and sketch a simple schema for your most important entities. Then run a small pilot where you generate and validate data for that schema before you try to automate anything site-wide.
